narrative: A weaker-than-expected US Nonfarm Payrolls print dominates the weekend tape, dialing back near-term Fed rate-hike expectations and setting up a soft USD profile for the Tokyo open. GBP/USD closed Friday at 1.3350, holding its weekly gains as Fed tightening doubts deepen, while EUR/USD closed Friday at 1.1440. The RBNZ rate decision on July 8 stands as the earliest high-impact central bank risk next week, where any hawkish delivery would support NZD from its Friday close of 0.5712.
